info.title: Holded API
info.description:
# Welcome to the Holded API

The Holded API allows you to integrate your business tools with the Holded ERP platform. Manage invoices, contacts, taxes, users, and more programmatically.

## Authentication

All requests require a valid API key. Include it in your request headers:

```
Authorization: Bearer <your-api-key>
```

## Conventions

- **Format:** JSON (`application/json`) for requests and responses
- **Rate limiting:** 100 requests per minute per API key
- **Pagination:** Use `page` and `limit` query parameters
- **Errors:** Standard HTTP status codes with descriptive error messages

get.description:
**Required permission:** `accounting:banks.read`

Returns a paginated list of transactions across all treasury accounts.

Results are cursor-paginated: the response includes a `cursor` value and a `has_more` boolean. Default page size is 25, configurable up to 100 with `limit`. Use this endpoint for a consolidated view of all cash movements.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of transactions to return per page. Defaults to 25, maximum 100.

treasury_id.description: Identifier of the treasury account this transaction belongs to

contact_name.description: Name of the contact associated with the transaction

date.description: Transaction date as an ISO 8601 timestamp

get.description:
**Required permission:** `sales:invoices.read`

Returns the list of files attached to a credit note.

Each attachment includes its identifier, file name, and size.

post.summary: Attach a file to a credit note
post.description:

Attaches a file to a credit note. Send the file as `multipart/form-data`.

Maximum file size is 10 MB.

Results are cursor-paginated: the response includes a `cursor` value and a `has_more` boolean. Default page size is 25, configurable up to 100 with `limit`.

post.description:
**Required permission:** `sales:invoices.write`

Creates a new credit note for a contact. Credit notes reduce the balance owed by a customer — typically issued to correct an error, apply a discount, or cancel a previously issued invoice.

You must provide either a `contact_id` or a `contact_name`. Include line items in the `items` array. The response returns the new credit note `id`.

post.summary: Create a payment for a credit note
post.description:
**Required permission:** `sales:invoices.write`

Records a payment against the credit note. The payment amount is deducted from the pending balance.

You can record partial payments by specifying an amount less than the total outstanding. The credit note status is automatically updated to paid when the pending balance reaches zero.

put.description:
**Required permission:** `sales:invoices.write`

Replaces all mutable fields of an existing credit note. This is a full replacement — any field you omit will be reset to its default value.

To avoid accidentally clearing data, first fetch the credit note with GET, modify the fields you need, and send back the full object.

delete.description:
**Required permission:** `sales:invoices.write`

The document number will not be reused by the numbering series.

**Required permission:** `sales:invoices.read`

The PDF is generated using the template configured in your account settings.

post.description:

You can optionally specify recipient email addresses, subject, and message body.

get.description:
**Required permission:** `sales:invoices.read`

Returns the list of files attached to an invoice.

Each attachment includes its identifier, file name, and size. Use the attachment ID to download or delete individual files.

post.summary: Attach a file to an invoice
post.description:
**Required permission:** `sales:invoices.write`

Attaches a file to an invoice. Send the file as `multipart/form-data`.

Maximum file size is 10 MB. Attached files are visible in the invoice detail view and can be downloaded by your team. They are not included in emails sent to the contact.

get.description:
**Required permission:** `sales:invoices.read`

Returns a paginated list of invoices in your account.

Results are cursor-paginated: the response includes a `cursor` value and a `has_more` boolean. Pass the `cursor` to the next request to fetch the following page. The default page size is 25 invoices, configurable up to 100 with the `limit` parameter.

The list includes payment summary fields (`payments_total`, `payments_pending`) so you can display outstanding balances without fetching each invoice individually.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of invoices to return per page. Defaults to 25, maximum 100.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Opaque cursor for pagination. Use the value returned in the previous response to fetch the next page.

post.description:
**Required permission:** `sales:invoices.write`

Creates a new sales invoice for a contact. Invoices track amounts owed to you by customers and are the primary document for revenue recognition.

You must provide either a `contact_id` (existing contact) or a `contact_name` (matches or creates a contact automatically). Include line items in the `items` array with product/service details, quantities, and prices. Taxes are calculated automatically based on the tax IDs provided.

The response returns the new invoice `id`. The invoice is created in draft status unless your account settings auto-approve new documents. Use the numbering series (`number_line_id`) to control the document number format.

contact_id.description: Identifier of an existing contact

contact_name.description: Name of the contact. Used to match or create a contact if contact_id is not provided.

date.description: Issue date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD)

due_date.description: Payment due date in ISO 8601 format

notes.description: Internal notes visible only to your team

number_line_id.description: Identifier of the numbering series to use

warehouse_id.description: Identifier of the warehouse for stock movements

discount.description: Global discount percentage to apply

project_id.description: Identifier of the project to link

payment_method_id.description: Identifier of the default payment method

post.summary: Create a payment for an invoice
post.description:
**Required permission:** `sales:invoices.write`

Records a payment against the invoice. The payment amount is deducted from the pending balance.

You can record partial payments by specifying an amount less than the total outstanding. Each payment is linked to a treasury account (`bank_id`) for cash-flow tracking. The invoice status is automatically updated to paid when the pending balance reaches zero.

**Required permission:** `sales:invoices.write`

Replaces all mutable fields of an existing invoice. This is a full replacement — any field you omit from the request body will be reset to its default value.

To avoid accidentally clearing data, first fetch the invoice with GET, modify the fields you need, and send back the full object. Partial updates are not supported. Updating an invoice does not affect payments already recorded against it.

delete.description:
**Required permission:** `sales:invoices.write`

Permanently deletes an invoice. This action cannot be undone.

If the invoice has recorded payments, they will also be removed. Deleting an invoice does not affect the contact or any other documents. The document number will not be reused by the numbering series.

get.description:
**Required permission:** `sales:invoices.read`

Downloads the invoice as a PDF file. The response Content-Type is `application/pdf`.

The PDF is generated using the template configured in your account settings. It reflects the current state of the invoice, including any updates made after creation.

post.description:
**Required permission:** `sales:invoices.write`

Sends the invoice by email to the contact. The email uses the template and sender address configured in your account settings.

You can optionally specify recipient email addresses, subject, and message body. If omitted, the defaults from your email template are used. The contact does not need to have an email address if you provide one in the request.

put.description:
**Required permission:** `sales:invoices.write`

Moves the invoice to a specific pipeline stage. Pipelines let you track invoices through custom workflows (e.g. Draft → Sent → Paid).

You must provide the `pipeline_id` of the target stage. The invoice's status is updated accordingly.

post.description:
**Required permission:** `sales:estimates.write`

Once accepted, the estimate can be converted into an invoice, sales order, or other document.

get.description:
**Required permission:** `contacts:contacts.read`

Returns a paginated list of contacts in your account.

You can filter by `phone`, `mobile`, `email`, or `custom_id` to find specific contacts. All filters use exact matching. Results are cursor-paginated: the response includes a `cursor` value and a `has_more` boolean. Pass the `cursor` to the next request to fetch the following page.

The default page size is 25 contacts. You can increase it up to 100 using the `limit` parameter.

parameters.name: phone
parameters.description: Filter contacts by exact phone number match

parameters.name: mobile
parameters.description: Filter contacts by exact mobile number match

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Filter contacts by exact email address match

parameters.name: custom_id
parameters.description: Filter contacts by your external reference identifier

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor for pagination. Use the value returned in the previous response to fetch the next page.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of contacts to return per page. Defaults to 25, maximum 100.

post.description:
**Required permission:** `contacts:contacts.write`

Creates a new contact in your account. Contacts represent the people and companies you do business with — customers, suppliers, leads, debtors, or creditors.

At minimum you must provide a `name`. The `type` field controls how the contact appears in your accounting (e.g. client, supplier). If omitted, the contact is created without a type and can be classified later.

The response returns the new contact `id`. Use it to create invoices, purchases, or any other document linked to this contact.

get.description:
**Required permission:** `contacts:contacts.read`

Retrieves the full details of a single contact by its unique identifier.

The response includes all contact data: billing address, shipping addresses, contact persons, custom fields, default invoicing settings, social networks, and accounting records. Use this endpoint when you need the complete contact profile — for list views, prefer the List contacts endpoint which returns the same fields but paginated.

put.description:
**Required permission:** `contacts:contacts.write`

Replaces all mutable fields of an existing contact. This is a full replacement — any field you omit from the request body will be reset to its default value.

To avoid accidentally clearing data, first fetch the contact with GET, modify the fields you need, and send back the full object. Partial updates are not supported on this endpoint.

delete.description:
**Required permission:** `contacts:contacts.write`

Permanently deletes a contact from your account. This action cannot be undone.

Existing documents (invoices, estimates, etc.) linked to this contact will retain the contact name for display purposes, but the live association is removed. You will not be able to filter documents by this contact after deletion.

get.description:
**Required permission:** `inventory:products.read`

Returns a paginated list of products in your catalog.

Results are cursor-paginated: the response includes a `cursor` value and a `has_more` boolean. Default page size is 25, configurable up to 100 with `limit`.

post.description:
**Required permission:** `inventory:products.write`

Creates a new product in your catalog. Products track inventory, pricing, and tax configuration. They can be physical goods with stock tracking or non-stockable items.

The response returns the new product `id`. You can then use this ID when adding line items to invoices, sales orders, and other documents.

name.description: Product name

price.description: Unit sale price as a decimal string

cost.description: Unit cost price as a decimal string

tags.description: List of tag names

taxes.description: List of tax identifiers to apply

for_sale.description: Whether the product is available for sale

for_purchase.description: Whether the product is available for purchase

get.description:

Retrieves the full details of a single product by its unique identifier.

The response includes variants, stock levels across warehouses, custom fields, tax configuration, and pricing information.

put.description:
**Required permission:** `inventory:products.write`

Replaces all mutable fields of an existing product. This is a full replacement — any field you omit will be reset to its default value.

To avoid accidentally clearing data, first fetch the product with GET, modify the fields you need, and send back the full object. Stock levels are managed separately via the stock endpoint.

name.description: Product name

price.description: Unit sale price as a decimal string

cost.description: Unit cost price as a decimal string

tags.description: List of tag names

taxes.description: List of tax identifiers to apply

for_sale.description: Whether the product is available for sale

for_purchase.description: Whether the product is available for purchase

Permanently deletes a product from your catalog. This action cannot be undone.

Products referenced in existing documents will retain the product name in line items but lose the live association. Stock records for this product are also removed.

put.description:
**Required permission:** `inventory:products.write`

Adjusts the stock level of a product in a specific warehouse. You can set an absolute value or apply a relative adjustment.

Stock changes are recorded as movements for traceability. If the product has variants, you must specify the variant ID.

warehouse_id.description: Warehouse ID

stock_variation.description: Stock quantity to add (positive) or remove (negative)

The response returns the new treasury `id`. You must specify a `type` (bank, card, gateway, or cash) and optionally provide bank details like IBAN and BIC.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code of the account

Consider archiving the account instead if you want to hide it from active views while preserving the transaction history.

get.description:
**Required permission:** `accounting:taxes.read`

Returns the complete list of taxes configured in your account. Taxes define the VAT, GST, or other rates applied to document line items.

Use this endpoint to discover available tax IDs before creating documents. Each tax includes its percentage rate, type, and applicable region.

**Required permission:** `inventory:warehouses.read`

Returns the list of all warehouses in your account.

The response includes each warehouse's name, address, and default status. Unlike document endpoints, warehouses are not paginated — all warehouses are returned in a single response.

post.description:
**Required permission:** `inventory:warehouses.write`

Creates a new warehouse in your account. Warehouses are locations where you store inventory — each product's stock is tracked per warehouse.

The response returns the new warehouse `id`. The first warehouse created is automatically set as the default.

get.description:
**Required permission:** `inventory:warehouses.read`

Retrieves the details of a single warehouse by its unique identifier.

The response includes the warehouse name, contact information, address, and whether it is the default warehouse.

put.description:
**Required permission:** `inventory:warehouses.write`

Updates an existing warehouse. You can change the name, contact information, and address.

The default warehouse flag cannot be changed via this endpoint.

delete.description:
**Required permission:** `inventory:warehouses.write`

Permanently deletes a warehouse. You cannot delete a warehouse that still holds stock.

Transfer all stock to another warehouse before deleting. You also cannot delete the default warehouse.

get.summary: Get stock levels for a warehouse
get.description:
**Required permission:** `inventory:warehouses.read`

Returns the stock levels for all products stored in a specific warehouse.

The response includes each product's ID, variant ID (if applicable), and current stock quantity. Use this endpoint to audit inventory or synchronize stock with external systems.
